name: Ascent to Lorigas from Benizar — circular through the Sierra de Moratalla
name_original: Subida a las Lorigas desde Benizar — circular por la Sierra de Moratalla
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Moratalla traverse a landscape characterized by rugged mountains, scenic river valleys, and diverse agricultural lands. The region features significant elevation changes, with prominent ranges like the Sierra de Moratalla and Revolcadores Mountains offering demanding climbs and descents. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including sections through pine groves, past river courses, and through open fields. This topography provides a range of road cycling experiences, from challenging mountain ascents to more moderate routes through valleys. Best road cycling routes around Moratalla The most popular road cycling route is Safe Cycling Route - Itinerary 7, a 43.1 miles (69.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 21 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ascent to Lorigas from Benizar — circular through the Sierra de Moratalla, a difficult 44.0 miles (70.8 km) path. This route offers challenging climbs and panoramic views within the Sierra de Moratalla. Local road cyclists also love the Moratalla – Caravaca de la Cruz loop from Moratalla, a 21.5 miles (34.7 km) trail leading through the region's diverse landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 38 minutes. Road cycling around Moratalla is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and agricultural lands. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in Moratalla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Moratalla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Moratalla?
A: Moratalla offers a diverse selection of over 20 road cycling routes. These routes cater to various skill levels, with an equal number of moderate and difficult options, ensuring there's a suitable challenge for most cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Moratalla?
A: Road cycling in Moratalla is characterized by its mountainous terrain, featuring significant elevation changes with demanding climbs and exhilarating descents through ranges like the Sierra de Moratalla and Revolcadores Mountains. You'll also encounter scenic river valleys, such as the Alhárabe River, and picturesque agricultural lands dotted with almond and pine groves, offering a varied and visually appealing experience.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Moratalla is known for its challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009145498/ascent-to-lorigas-from-benizar-circular-through-the-sierra-de-moratalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Lorigas from Benizar — circular through the Sierra de Moratalla&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 70.8 km path with over 1200 meters of elevation gain, offering panoramic views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20127018" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – Letur Historic Center loop from Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;, which spans over 104 km with more than 1600 meters of ascent.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Moratalla's routes offer stunning natural beauty. You can cycle past majestic peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4490752" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de los Obispos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5389729" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Lorigas Summit&lt;/a&gt;, or explore impressive gorges such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5616929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Taibilla River&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696563" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañón de los Almacenes&lt;/a&gt;. During certain seasons, you might even ride through fragrant lavender fields in the highlands.

Q: Are there any moderate or shorter road cycling routes suitable for a less intense ride?
A: Absolutely. For a more moderate experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – Caravaca de la Cruz loop from Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;. This route is about 34.7 km long with around 440 meters of elevation gain, typically completed in under two hours. Another moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31883304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caravaca de la Cruz – Moratalla loop from Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;, covering 57.1 km with about 670 meters of ascent.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Moratalla?
A: The road cycling routes in Moratalla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain ascents in the Sierra de Moratalla to tranquil rides through river valleys and agricultural lands. The variety of terrain and the rewarding panoramic views are frequently highlighted.

Q: When is the best time of year to go road cycling in Moratalla?
A: Moratalla offers good cycling conditions for much of the year. However, autumn is particularly recommended, not only for pleasant temperatures but also for the unique experience of cycling through the vast lavender fields in the highlands. Spring also offers mild weather and blooming landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of Moratalla's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1009145498/ascent-to-lorigas-from-benizar-circular-through-the-sierra-de-moratalla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Lorigas from Benizar — circular through the Sierra de Moratalla&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38185927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moratalla – Caravaca de la Cruz loop from Moratalla&lt;/a&gt;, providing convenient options for exploration.

Q: Is it possible to rent road bikes or join guided tours in Moratalla?
A: Yes, services like Cicloraíces offer guided cycling tours specifically in the Sierra de Moratalla, and they also provide bike rentals. This makes it convenient for visitors who prefer not to bring their own equipment or wish to explore the area with local expertise.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or 'miradores' along the routes?
A: Throughout the road cycling routes in Moratalla, you'll find numerous 'miradores' or viewpoints. These strategically located spots offer stunning vistas of the region's diverse landscape, including sweeping views of almond and pine groves, and the winding river valleys.
tips: The highest point of the port where a mythical climb for road bike lovers ends. The end is somewhat irregular, but we could say that the climb ends at the height of a sign that informs about the passage of animals.
An essential place to visit in early summer to see the Lavender Fields.

In this place, located about 7 km from the town of Moratalla and on the slopes of Pico de los Frailes, it is said that the "appearance" of Jesus Christ took place in 1493. From the viewpoint we will have impressive views of the territory of Moratalla and neighboring towns, highlighting the Sierra del Buitre, the Sierra del Cerezo and the historic center of Moratalla.

Passage area for many road cyclists who are encouraged to go up to Los Álamos and the lavender fields. This monument was built on the outskirts of the town of Moratalla and it is a religious symbol that symbolizes the arrival of the Virgin and the Lord on the pilgrimage.
